title: Board Observer Agreement
description: Grants an investor or institutional representative the right to attend board meetings as a non-voting observer.
whatsCovered: Observer right to receive board packages and notices
Non-voting status and exclusion from executive board votes
Withholding rights for attorney-client privilege files
Exclusion from meetings involving competitor audits
whenToUse: Use this agreement when a venture fund invests heavily and wants to sit in on board discussions without taking on director fiduciary duties.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

fields.label: Observer Exclusions
fields.placeholder: Describe topics where the observer must leave the room (e.g. competitor acquisitions, salary discussions)...

clauses.heading: BOARD OBSERVER AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for Board Observer Agreement. The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. Board Meetings Access
clauses.body: Observer {observer_name} representing {investor_firm} is invited to attend board meetings. Observer shall be excluded from discussions concerning: {observer_scope}.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Does a board observer vote?
faq.answer: No. Observers have no voting authority and are not considered directors under corporate law.

faq.question: Can observers be excluded from meetings?
faq.answer: Yes. The board can exclude observers to protect attorney-client privilege or during highly sensitive competitor reviews.
